title: asplenium fontanum subsp. foresiacum
intro: Asplenium fontanum subsp. foresiacum, also known as the spleenwort or maidenhair spleenwort, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to Europe, particularly in the mountainous regions of the Alps and the Pyrenees. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and acting as an anti-oxidant. In traditional herbal medicine, Asplenium fontanum subsp. foresiacum is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or salve.
